name: Ext.Http
comment:
This module extends the `Http` module with better error handling and more information in the success case.

@docs Error, TaskInput, errorString, identityResolver, jsonResolver

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: Use Ext.Http.Error instead of Http.Error to get more information about the error.

aliases.name: TaskInput
aliases.comment: Named type alias for the `Http.task` parameter
aliases.type: { method : String.String, headers : List.List Http.Header, url : String.String, body : Http.Body, resolver : Http.Resolver x a, timeout : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float }

values.name: errorString
values.comment: Convenience function to convert an Ext.Http.Error to a String.

values.name: identityResolver
values.comment:
A resolver that does process the response at all. It just returns the response as is, but with `Http.Metadata` included. And with error type as `Ext.Http.Error` instead of `Http.Error`.

Useful for decoding non-json payloads like Bytes or String
values.type: Http.Response a -> Result.Result (Ext.Http.Error a) { meta : Http.Metadata, data : a }

values.name: jsonResolver
values.comment:
Resolver for `Http.task` that includes proper details in both the error case and metadata in the success case.

Usually metadata is not needed in the success case, just add `Task.map .data` to your existing code

    ```diff
     Http.task
         { method = "GET"
         , headers = []
         , url = "https://example.com"
         , body = Http.emptyBody
    -    , resolver = Http.stringResolver (otherJsonResolver myDecoder)
    +    , resolver = Http.stringResolver (Ext.Http.jsonResolver myDecoder)
         , timeout = Nothing
         }
    +    |> Task.map .data
    ```

But when you need it, having the metadata available is very useful.

    ```diff
     Http.task
         { method = "GET"
         , headers = []
         , url = "https://example.com"
         , body = Http.emptyBody
         , resolver = Http.stringResolver (Ext.Http.jsonResolver myDecoder)
         , timeout = Nothing
         }
    +    |> Task.map (Debug.log "{ meta, data }")
         |> Task.map .data
    ```
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Http.Response String.String -> Result.Result (Ext.Http.Error String.String) { meta : Http.Metadata, data : a }
